text: Swae Lee's Coachella's Set Reportedly Cut Off Before 'Black Beatles' - TMZ
fullText: Sorry to Swae Lee fans ... they missed out on his hit "Black Beatles" when his Coachella set on Friday night was seemingly cut off!

text: Mamdani’s administration scrambles on key appointment - Politico
fullText: Pushback over the mayor’s Department of Investigation pick, Nadia Shihata, is part of a widening spat with the City Council.

text: Live Updates: U.S. official says no agreements have been made in Iran peace talks yet as shaky ceasefire holds - CBS News
fullText: U.S. and Iranian negotiators have not yet made any agreements during high-stakes negotiations amid a fragile two-week truce.

text: RFK Jr. has turned corporate America’s name to mud, POLITICO Poll finds - Politico
fullText: Most Americans, especially Republicans, want to regulate food and drug makers now.

text: India cracks down on satirists for turning its prime minister into a punch line - NPR
fullText: India's satirists are turning Prime Minister Narendra Modi into a punch line — and the government is hitting back.

text: Gut troubles? This gastroenterologist has tips to help you achieve 'poophoria' - NPR
fullText: In her new book You've Been Pooping All Wrong, Dr. Trisha Pasricha shares habits and practices to make your relationship with your solid waste as smooth as possible

text: Woman Diagnosed with Vulvar, Cervical and Anal Cancer After Learning Her Husband of 30 Years Had Cheated on Her - Yahoo Lifestyle Singapore
fullText: A Florida woman learned that her husband of 30 years had been cheating on her and that he had given her HPV. Soon after, she was diagnosed with cancer of the vulva, cervix and anus, which are linked the virus.

text: An Orban loss in Hungary’s election could be the turning point Putin fears - France 24
fullText: Hungary's legislative elections on Sunday are being closely watched in Moscow. Prime Minister Viktor Orban has long been Russian President Vladimir Putin's closest ally within the European Union, and a victory for the opposition Tisza party led by Peter Magya…

text: Peru's election: A battle for the Presidency amid political chaos and crime - NPR
fullText: With 35 candidates in the race, Peru is set to elect its 9th president in less than a decade. Amid rising corruption and crime, voters are left asking: Can this election finally break the cycle?

text: How AI is getting better at finding security holes - NPR
fullText: Anthropic announced this week that its new model found security flaws in "every major operating system and web browser." Even before the news, AI models had gotten dramatically better at finding bugs.

text: Anthropic’s latest AI model strikes fear into banks - Morning Brew
fullText: Mythos required an emergency meeting between government officials and bank leaders

text: Marriage status has surprising link to cancer risk, study suggests: 'Clear signal' - AOL.com
fullText: Never-married adults are significantly more likely to develop cancer, according to a University of Miami study of over 4 million people in 12 states.

text: The 'affordability economy' has created a housing market nobody predicted: Prices collapsing in the Sun Belt, soaring in the Rust Belt - Fortune
fullText: Las Vegas, Austin, and Miami are now leading a national price downturn, while Midwest metros are thriving.

text: Artemis II splashdown: Astronauts home safely after journey around Moon - BBC
fullText: Four astronauts splashed down in the Pacific after a nine-day voyage that took them further from Earth than any humans.

text: Anyma’s Coachella Set Canceled Due to ‘Strong Wind Conditions’ - Variety
fullText: Anyma's Coachella performance has been scrapped due to severe weather conditions. It is unclear whether the DJ's set will be rescheduled.

text: Trump says plans filed for huge triumphal arch in Washington - DW.com
fullText: The US arch would be over 50% taller than the famous Arc de Triomphe in Paris, according to the official plans. The US president has described the project as "the GREATEST and MOST BEAUTIFUL Triumphal Arch" in the world.

text: Sabrina Carpenter Headlines Coachella, Bringing Sexy Pop Hits, Old Hollywood Flare and a Bizarre Susan Sarandon Monologue to the Desert - Variety
fullText: Sabrina Carpenter brought sex appeal, silliness, a bizarre Susan Sarandon cameo and big hits to her headlining Coachella performance.

text: Exclusive: US intelligence indicates China is preparing weapons shipment to Iran amid fragile ceasefire, sources say - CNN
fullText: US intelligence indicates that China is preparing to deliver new air defense systems to Iran within the next few weeks, according to three people familiar with recent intelligence assessments.
